rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DARLENE J FULLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: The ring was not appraised by the Probate Referee and it does not appear that the conservator applied for a waiver of such appraisal. [Probate Code Sections 2610(c), 8902, and 8903.] The I&A filed on 9/18/15 also listed all assets as cash, but the pension and annuity were never appraised by the Probate Referee. Schedule A (Receipts) and Schedule C (Disbursements) do not include full descriptions, just the source or payee or just the description, instead of both [Probate Code Section 1062.] Reimbursements to anyone, especially the fiduciary, must indicate who the original payee was. Which prior Court order referenced in paragraph 13 of this petition authorized the conservator to reimburse himself for travel and other costs? An account must state the date of any order authorizing payment of fiduciary or attorney fees. See Judicial Council (not Counsel) form GC-400(C)(4). Court filing fees ($1,000) paid to the attorney on 9/4/20 must be itemized. Why does the conservatorship pay a post office box fee? The assets on hand at the end of the supplemental accounting period appear to be incorrect as funds were reportedly transferred out of Fifth Third Bank. Transfer of cash between accounts is not a change in the form of assets. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] Only the original financial statements showing the account balance as of the closing date of the court accounting is required for interim or final accounts. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/22/21. After the issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required in connection with the attorney fees and costs. Some of the time spent to address issues with the second accounting appear to have been necessitated by accounting or drafting errors or the misreading of an order increasing bond. It is unclear why certified Letters could not be obtained or the Notice of Death could not be filed via mail or a messenger service. Most of the time spent to rectify the investment of funds outside of the state should not have been necessary, had the attorney recognized the issue before the Court did. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (The conservator filed his second account on 10/31/18, not 11/14/18, and it was allowed, not approved. The petition for letters of administration was filed on 9/14/20, not 11/24/20, and that petition was granted on 12/21/20, not 12/22/20. References in this petition to the decedent's estate or the administrator (paragraphs 20-24) do not apply to this proceeding and will be disregarded. There is no need to include a deceased person on the service list for notice.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF SU HWA WAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No ex parte petition has been filed yet to increase bond. The bond calculation shown in the third accounting petition appears to be based on the carry value, not the market value, of assets on hand. The accounting cannot be approved until proof of sufficient bond is on file. (Refunds that were likely just one-time receipts do not need to be included in the annual income.) There were some significantly higher payments to the caregiving company (such as on 2/4/19 and 10/22/19) which need further clarification. (It would be helpful if the description of the disbursements showed the periods covered by such payments.) The order dated 8/1/18 authorized up to $10,000 in fees to the litigation attorney, subject to Court approval. The order dated 6/17/19 authorized $5,666.68. Was the latter amount not part of the $10,000? This accounting shows two payments exceeding $10,000. The last item on the Gains schedule does not appear to be correct or appears to be the incorrect number of shares sold for the incorrect holding if item d. under non-cash items on Schedule E is correct. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/30/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WAYNE THOMAS PETERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Whether to reduce the Executor compensation by $396.00 for payment made for a 1-hour consultation with a probate attorney. 2) Attorney requested $7,840.00 in extraordinary compensation. Examiner requested timesheets for ordinary services so the court can determine whether extraordinary fees are "just and reasonable" (Prob. C. 10811(a)). Attorney alleges they did not keep timesheets for ordinary services. 3) Attorney is requesting $3,763.08 in costs. Attorney alleges $1,554.50 was spent on Swift Attorney Services for filing and delivery fees that could have been avoided by mailing documents and filing with file and serve express. 4) Whether the estate should pay for entry 10/6/2018 Dinner for 34 people plus tip $2,501.05. 5) $12.00 was reimbursed to Gail Yoshida for parking. Parking is absorbed as part of fees [LRSF 14.58 B.] 6) Examiner requested a receipt for entry 5/23 Estevan Lopez Landscaping for $250.00. Petitioner has not provided a receipt.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GAUALOFA M. CASEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/2021. 1) 4th amended account end 04/09/2021 please provide bank statements that verify the balance of the accounts at the end of the account period 04/09/2021. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Ending balances shown in the account must be reconciled to the statements or letters, which must be filed. Unless filed confidentially in a guardianship or conservatorship case, account numbers should be redacted as set forth in CRC 1.201. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 2) Please provide an updated proposed order that matches what is requested in the 4th amended petition. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/14/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER DAVID ANDRADE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/13/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A.] If original bank statements were not E-filed with the petition, they must be filed prior to the continued hearing. [LRSF 14.35 D.] Whenever originals are E-filed, the attorney or the fiduciary who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4., which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/30/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DONALD L. YOST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining That Certain Assets Are Included In Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Despite the tentative rulings having repeatedly noted that NO COURTESY COPIES have been received by the assigned Examiner and that the wrong form of notice had been served, and the Examiner having noted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 3/9/21 that a copy of the trust does not appear to have been attached to the petition, and Counsel stating at the hearing on 4/13/21 that the documents (supplement or amendment to the trust, as appropriate, plus the proof of service of the correct Notice of Hearing) would get filed, nothing has been filed as of 5/25/21 and no courtesy copies have been received. Additionally, it appears that notice of the initial hearing on 2/9/21 was mailed on 1/15/21, which was not at least 30 days before the hearing, so notice was defective even to the parties served, whether or not they are all of the parties entitled to notice. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BASSAM ELIAS JAJEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/6/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Notwithstanding these rules, the petition was skimmed online as a one-time courtesy in hopes of avoiding a continuance. Item 8 did not show the RELATIONSHIPS to the decedent of the persons listed; this information was set forth in another attachment to the petition (which should not have been necessary, had item 8 been complete). The dates of death of the decedent's parents must be set forth in a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner. [LRSF 14.18 C. and CRC 7.103(c).] The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete at item 2 (no telephone number). Courtesy copies of the petition and all supporting papers, including the supplement and the new, complete Duties and Liabilities, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/24/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL WISNYI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/2021. 1) Please prove the will as the Will does not appear to be not self-proving under Probate Code 8220(b) and CCP 2015.5. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/14/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: TRUST FOR MICHAEL SWORDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #14, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/29/2021. 1) Please explain and provide legal authority for why a petition for final distribution has not been filed as Decedent died 06/11/2019 and the account ends 12/31/2020. 2) Please explain why Schedule C alleges total income for 2012, 2013, and 2014 when this account alleges it is from January 1, 2018 to December 31, 2020. 3) Please provide legal authority for notice to Teresa Swords c/o Mardi B. Swords. Please explain why direct notice has not been given to Teresa Swords. Please explain Mardi B. Swords relation to Michael Swords. 4) Please explain the payments to Mario Alfonso what acts were performed and how it was a benefit to Michael Swords. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/14/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
